text: not annotated
not annotated
The title CATCH ME IF YOU CAN appears on screen.
The words Inspired by a true story fade into the screen as the sound of an audience applauding begins.
The scene opens to a game show where the words To Tell The Truth occupy the camera. The main game show host introduces the program with "Thank you very much, and welcome to To Tell the Truth."

The doors on the back of the show set open as three men in pilot uniforms walk out onto stage. The host begins with "Our first guest. He's made a career out of being the most outrageous imposter that we've ever come across on this show. And you're going to see what I mean."

The game show introduces the contestants: "Number one, what is your name please?". The camera zooms in on contestant number one as he says "My name is Frank William Abignail." The camera moves to contestant number 2 as the host prompts him: "Number two." The middle guy responds: "My name is Frank William Abignail." Finally on the hosts words of  "Number three" the camera zooms in on contestant number 3 to which the contestant replies "My name is Frank William Abignail."

The host begins to describe the imposter as the camera zooms in on the first contestant. He says, "From 1964 to 1967 I successfully impersonated an airline pilot for Pan Am Airways and I flew over two million miles for free..."
The camera moves to the second contestant as the host continues: "..During that time, I was also the chief resident pediatrician at a Georgia hospital..."
The camera moves to the third contestant as the host adds "..and an assistant attorney general for the State of Louisiana..."

The camera zooms out to view all three contestants while the host says "...By the time I was caught, I was considered the youngest and most daring con man in U.S. history. I had cashed almost $4 million in fraudulent checks in 26 foreign countries and all 50 states. And I did it all..."
The contestants move over to their seats as the host finishes with "...before my 19th birthday. My name is Frank William Abagnail."
The audience applauses. The three contestants reach their seats and sit down.
The camera cuts to the host. He explains "So, for the first time he's going to have to tell the truth and we're going to start our questioning with Kitty."
The camera cuts to kitty as she begins speaking: "Thank you. Number One, why, with all your talent, and you're obviously a very bright fellow, why didn't you go in for a..."

As Kitty continues the camera moves to cuts to contestant number one. She finishes "...legitimate profession?" Contestant number one responds with "It was really a question of dollars and cents. When I was a young man, I needed the money and I thought this list of careers was the easiest way to get it."

Kitty responds with "I see." The camera moves to the second contestant as she asks "Number Two, I find this all very fascinating. Who was it that finally caught you?" The second contestant leans into the mike and answers "His name was Carl Hanratty."
The scene cuts to one of men standing outside in the rain. In a French accent the man keep repeating "Han-an-an-ratty. Ratty."
The camera moves to a view of all the men who are talking. In an American accent one man replies "Han-ratty. Carl Hanratty."
The camera cuts to a close up of one of the man's mouth as he enunciates "Yes. -I... am... Carl Hanratty."

Next, the camera shows a view of Hanratty's back as he continues to speak to the French men. He states, "I represent the FBI from the United States of America. Yeah. I have orders to see the American prisoner, Abagnale." The French men continue to speak during this time to each other in French.
The camera is now a wide shot of the men talking. The words Marseille, France Christmas Eve, 1969 appear on screen.
Through a hole in the door three men can be seen. The first one opens the lock to the door.
The men begin to walk through the open door and down a dark hallway. During the walk the first man grabs a chair and places down at the end of the hallway.
As Hanratty walks over to the first man, the french man explains "You sit here. You do not open the door. You do not pass him..."
The French officer opens a latch on the door and continues "anything through the hole." Then the French officer walks away to speak to his colleague as Hanratty sits on the stool.
Through the gate of the window a close of Hanratty is shown. He says "Aw... Jesus.", as loud coughing can be heard from the cell.
The camera cuts to a view of the prisoner in the cell. He slowly sinks down to the floor all the while coughing.

Another view of Hanratty is shown from his back. He gets up from the stool and turns around to see one of the prison guards standing there. He looks up to hear thunder and drips coming from the ceiling.
Hanratty opens his umbrella before sitting down on the stool again.
The camera cuts back to the prisoner inside the cell. His coughing gets worse as he gags.
Hanratty fills the screen as the camera cuts back to him. He tells the prisoner "You know, I've got a little bit of a cold myself."
The camera cuts back to the prisoner as he wheezes and coughs more.
Hanratty is heard saying "Frank...I'm here to read the articles of extradition..."

The camera moves back to show Hanratty's back from a wide view of the hallway. He pulls something from his bag and begins to read "according to the European Court for Human Rights. Article One: Extradition..."
A cut to Hanratty's face the grated window appears as he continues "...shall be granted in respect of offenses punishable under the laws..."
The camera cuts to the prisoner. Weakly he mumbles "Help me." multiple times. Hanratty continues "...for the maximum period of at least one year of a severe penalty..."
The camera cuts to the view of Hanratty's face. He stops reading as Frank continues to mumble "Help me."
Hanratty says "Frank... stop it."
Frank's stare comes into view as he says "Help me" again.
Hanratty simply responds with "You don't think actually you can fool me, do you?"
Frank thumps back against the wall of the cell. Meanwhile, Hanratty says "Sixteen pages to go, stay with me."
The camera cuts back to Hanratty's face, he continues to read "Article Two: If..."
A shot of Hanratty with his umbrella outside cell appears. He reads "... the request for extradition includes several separate offenses each of which is punishable under the laws..."
The camera cuts back to Frank. He coughs some more then collapses. Hanratty reads "...of the requesting party...""
The camera cuts to Hanratty as he looks up. He asks "Frank?...Frank?"

A wide shot of the hallway is show as Hanratty gets up from the stool. He kicks the stool over and the French guard walks over. The camera zooms to Hanratty's face as he speaks: "Goddamn it! Get me a doctor in here! I need a doctor! Doctor! Now!"

The scene changes to one of the French guards carrying a sunken Frank down another hallway. Hanratty tells him "Don't sweat it, Frank. We're going to get you right to a doctor. Frank, if you can hear me, don't worry. I'm going to take you home in the morning. Home in the morning, Frank." The group reaches the hospital room where they deposit Frank face down on the bed.

The men exit the room. All of the French guards begin to wash their hands. Hanratty asks the guards "What are you doing?" One replies, "Washing off the lice." Hanratty begins to explain "This man has to be on a plane for America. He has to see a doctor." The French guard ignores his comments and states "The doctor comes in tomorrow."
Hanratty walks closer to the French man speaking and says "I have worked too long, too hard for you to take this away from me. If he dies, I'm holding you responsible.
The men turn to see a metal door begin to shut. One of the French guards walks over to it. He then walks over to the hospital curtain surrounding the bed.

The guard quickly opens the curtain to find that Frank is no longer on the bed. The guard yells to another "Monsieur!". All of the guards quickly run out of the room Hanratty remains and says "Ah Frank" to himself.
The scene opens to prisoners banging metal objects against a wire fence and yelling. Frank comes running slowly down the hallway while bending over and coughing.
Frank slows down and begins to fall. He quickly tries to recover and get on his feet.
Frank is unable to get up and slowly starts crawling forward. Guards appear in the background walking.
The camera cuts to the back of Frank. He glances back and notices the guards behind him. He tries to quicken his pace.
The camera cuts back to the guards. It follows them as they begin to approach Frank.
One official pulls out a gun and puts it to Frank's head. He get up onto his knees and looks up. Meanwhile, the guards yell at the other prisoners to keep quiet.
Frank throws his hands up and leans back giving up his escape.
Hanratty walks closer to Frank lying on the ground.

The camera cuts to Frank as he says "Okay, Carl...let's go home." The camera zooms out, and Frank turns to lay in his side. Sound from a man's voice overlaps the clips saying "The New Rochelle Rotary Club has a history that goes back to 1919."
THE RECORD title appears on screen
The camera zooms in from outside of a yellow house to see a girl by the window. Sounds of city traffic play.
A girl with red hair and a purple dress is sitting on a couch with a pizza box in her lap. She opens the pizza box, pulls out a slice, and raises it to her mouth about to take a bite.
Just about to eat the pizza, the girl hears a knock at the door. She then gets a puzzled look on her face.
The girl opens the door, looks around, and she sees no one there. Suddenly, she gets a surprised look on her face as she looks down.
We are shown a close up of the brown package at her feet.
She brings the package inside, and sits back down on her couch with the package on her lap. Then, she proceeds to open the package.
Within the package is a record titled A SINGLE LIFE. She takes it out of the sleeve.
The girl gets up from the couch and places the record on the record player.
The camera shows a close up of the record player. The girl places the arm on the disk, and the music starts to play.
The girl goes back to sitting on the couch where she proceeds to bop her head to the music. She picks up a slice of pizza from the box.

The record then skips. The girl looks at the record player suspiciously. It can be noticed that part of the pizza slice that she was eating has disappeared. She is about to take a bite of the pizza having decided to ignore the skipping of the record. Suddenly, her face becomes shocked as she looks down at the pizza slice.
There is a close up of the pizza which has bite marks in it, and it is halfway gone. The girl looks back at the record player with suspicion.
There is a close up of the record player spinning.
The girl has an idea, and begins to stop the record.
We are shown a close up of her hand on the record stopping the music.

The girl spins the record back and forth causing the music to skip around. As this occurs, her pizza slice reappears and disappears continuously. She then pauses for a second, and spins the record forward.

From the girls perspective, there is a close up of the couch, the pizza box, the clock, a carton of milk, and the candle. As the sound of the record being turned is heard, the handles of the clock move. Meanwhile, the pizza slices disappear, and the candle melts all rather quickly. Then as the record is being spun in the opposite direction, the pizza slices reappear, the handles of the clock move in the opposite direction, and the candle forms back together.
The girl spins the record one more time.
There is a close up of the girl's face with her mouth open and her eyes shut. All of a sudden, her eyes pop open, and she smiles.
She jumps off of the couch as the song plays, and she moves over to the record player.
We see another close up of the record player. She moves the arm more towards the middle the record, to a new position, and sets it down.

The same girl appears in a different room. She has aged, and is now wearing a black/white blazer with a black shirt. On the back wall of the apartment is what appears to be a wedding photo of her older self. She comes to the realization of where she is, and her face becomes surprised as she looks down at her belly.
There is a close up of her pregnant belly.
She has a realization of the record and her pregnancy.
She begins to thinks, and turns the record back and forth. As this occurs the size of her belly shrinks and grows. After a couple of spins back and forth, she spins the record quickly forward.
A baby in a blue blanket appears in her arms, and it begins to cry.
We come back to the close up of the record player. She moves the arm out on the record to a new position and sets it down.

The girl is now a child in a new room. She is wearing a red dress, and carrying a doll. Nursery music begins to play. She looks down at herself, frowns, and then drops the doll. Next, she moves over to the record player.

When we see the close up of the record player, it is shows that the girl is not tall enough to reach the arm on the record player. Her hand fumbles around until it knocks the arm over to a different spot.
The girl now finds herself as a middle aged women. She glances around to find herself with grey hair, a broken leg, and she is sitting in the wheelchair of a new room..
We see the close up of the girl's face as she sighs.

She tries to wheel herself over to the record player; however, each time she does this the record skips and she lands right back where she started. As this occurs, her movements become more feverish and insistent on reaching the record player.
We see a close up of her face as she frowns and her eyes narrow.
She is about to try once again to wheel herself over as the record skips. However, she accidentally tips her wheelchair backwards and falls.
We see a close up of the record player as the music skips.

The girl is now an old woman who is lying on the ground with a walker in front of her. She is in the room of her older self while wearing a blue floral dress and she has white hair. She picks herself up, examines herself/her surroundings, and shuffles over to the record player with the assistance of the walker.
There is a close up of the record player as the arm moves in on the record reaching the end of the song.
The scene skips time to the same room. However; this time the girl is not there. Instead, the walker she had carried falls over just as a lid falls on top of an urn to close it shut.
We are once again shown a close up of the record player. This time the arm moves itself off of the record, and lays itself to the resting position. The screen fades to black.
THE BOYFRIEND title appears on screen.
The title fades as words HIGH MAINTENANCE appear. Soft music begins to play.

The scene opens to a dining room where a man and woman are silently eating. The sounds of their chewing and utensils moving can be heard. The camera moves around the room starting from a close up of back of the man's head to show the woman he is heating with from across the table.
The camera turns to show the man eating.
The camera is a close up of the woman's face as she eats.
Again, the camera turns to show the man eating. This time, the view is even closer: one of just his face.
We are brought back to the view of the woman eating.
Once again, we are shown a close up of the man eating.

The camera widens to show a view of both the man and the woman. The woman puts her napkin down on the table and gets up. She grabs a bottle of wine off of the table and is about to pour it in the man's empty glass.
We see a close up of the wine glass as the man puts his hand over it. Then the camera cuts back to his face as he says "Not for me dear," and he proceeds to continue eating.
The camera shows just the woman as she sits back down in her seat. She sighs, looks at the man, and says "You know it's our anniversary."
The scene is now occupied by a view of the man from over the woman's shoulder. He pauses eating to say "You know I can't drink when I'm on call." Then he continues to take another bite of his food.
We see another close up of the front of the woman's face. She grabs her glass of wine and chugs what was left of it. She sighs loudly.
The woman places her glass back on the table. Then she grabs the wine bottle and pours herself some more.
The camera moves back to a view of the man from over the woman's shoulder. He is eating, but stops to say "The asparagus is very tender."
The is another close up of the front of the woman's face. She responds to the man: "Oh yeah."
The camera cuts back to the front of the man's face as he puts another bite of food in his mouth.
We see the woman's face in profile as a close up when she says "They say it's an aphrodisiac."
During another close up of the man's face in profile he asks "Who does?"

As we once again see a close up of the profile of the woman's face, she replies "Oh, I don't know. People". She eats another piece of food. While chewing she elaborates: "That's just what people say."
The camera cuts back and forth between closes up of the couple eating.
The man asks, "so dear, how was your day?"
The camera shows a view of the woman from over the man's shoulder. She scowls, pushes her chair back, and grabs her wine. As she stands, she yells "Oh geez..."
The woman throws the wine from her glass across the table, so that it hits the man, as she continues to yell"...shut up and have a drink..."
The man's face is seen up close with droplets of wine trailing down it. The woman can be heard saying "You're a computer analyst..."
The woman continues with "not a fucking surgeon" as the camera shows her sitting back down in her chair.

The camera briefly moves back to the man who doesn't respond. Next, the woman continues to speak: "Can't you just, I don't know, relax for once in your life." As she is speaking her folding her napkin back over her lap can be seen.
The woman looks up with an appalled face. She begins to admonish the man  "Oh don't you dare..."
We see the man bring a cigarette between his lips. The sound of the lighter can be heard.
The camera moves back to a shot of the woman as she continues speaking "...smoke in my house." She can be seen getting up from her chair.
A close up of the man is show with the now lit cigarette in his mouth. The woman's shoes can be heard walking over to him. Her hand appears as she takes the cigarette out of his mouth.
The woman's retreating form is shown as she walks back to her seat with the cigarette in hand.
The woman puts out the cigarette on an ash tray, which the camera zooms in on. She looks up from the cigarette to a sound of the man's lighter.
There is a close up of the man as he inhales a new lit cigarette and exhales the smoke.
The woman looks back at the man and begins to complain. As she returns to her seat she states, "Yeah, that's right. Pass the smoke. That's entirely what I wanted for my anniversary..."

The woman continues to complain as there is a close up of her face. All while resuming to eat again, she says "Followed by some stilted conversation. And if I'm really lucky by some short mechanical sex."
The man looks up at her, and says nothing. The woman remains silent as she chews her food.
The woman places her fork down. She begins to apologize: "I'm sorry."
The camera shows her back as she gets up an moves towards the man. She says "Come here," sits on his lap and continues to speak.
There is a close up of the faces of the man and the woman. The man has a blank expression as the woman speaks: "There. There. I didn't mean what I said."
The woman strokes the man's hair. We can see the back of his head as her fingers trail down to his neck. She pushes down his shirt collar to reveal a robot switch.
A close up of the woman's face portrays her sad face as she looks over the mans shoulder.
The camera shifts back to the close up of the man's neck. The woman's finger turns the switch off.
The man's face is blank as his chin falls onto the woman's shoulder bearing no weight of its own.
The woman sighs, and she pushes the man off of her. She gets up and returns to her side of the table. All the while the man's head sags and he lies motionless in the chair.
The woman sits back in her chair. She takes her shoes off and puts her feet on the table. She snidely looks at the man and says "Happy Anniversary"
We start the scene with the logo of a mac computer. The camera moves up to show the woman typing on the computer as piano music begins to play.
The screen of the computer is show. It reads PROMETEUS ROBOTICS CHOOSE EVERLASTING LOVE. The woman clicks on the website and pictures of men appear below their name and number.
The woman raises her eyebrow and resumes typing. She pauses and glances back into the dining room where the man still sits. She shifts back to the computer and smiles.
The screen shows one guy in particular named Paul with several photos of him.
The woman puts on an earphone and calls the number to the business. The soft music the was playing beings to fade.
The sound of a phone dialing begins. On the screen appears ...DIALING: 0800 800 800..

There is a woman on the other end of the line who speaks: "Hello you've reached Rache-line technical support. How can I help you?" The woman responds with "Hi, I'm unsatisfied with my current unit." The soft music resumes.

The lady on the line then asks "Okay, what model do you currently have?" As the woman responds with "It's the one-hundred series," her eyes trail over to her wedding photo. The lady on the line responds with "And what seems to be the problem?"

The camera cuts back to the woman on the phone. She tells the lady on the line "He lacks ambition. He has no sense of adventure." The lady on the line agrees: "Yes, that is a common malfunction with the one-hundred series. Would it help if we upgraded you to a higher model?"
The woman nods and answers "Yeah would you be able to give me something a bit sportier?" He gaze shifts back to the dinning room.
The lady on the line responds: "Certainly. Do you have any preferences?" The woman is quick to answer "Oh..um.. how about..."
There is a close up of the screen. Which shows pictures of Paul again. She continues to speak, saying "...a rock climber." She click on one of the photos of Paul and says "Oh, no. A masseuse."

The camera widens to show the woman sitting at her desk. She hastens to say "Oh wait. A rock climbing masseuse?" She pauses, and then answers the lady on the line to say "Yeah, like the picture." There is another short pause before the woman says "Yeah, but no beard. Maybe just a 5 o'clock shadow."
The camera zooms out to show the woman further and further away. She continues to speak on the phone: "Yeah and could he have shorter hair?" He words begin to fade as the camera zooms to black.
There is a knock on the door the ends the music. A close up of the woman looking into her peephole appears.

The woman opens the door to see a delivery woman and a large package being lowered from a dolly. The package says PROMETEUS ROBOTICS CHOOSE EVERLASTING LOVE. A second delivery woman, who lowered the package, moves in front of the door.
The delivery woman bring the package indoors, and they begin to rustle with the plastic that contains the package.
The woman stands with her mouth slightly open as she sees the plastic being unwrapped from the new man's face. A different music begins to play.
The woman turns to see the old boyfriend being moved out of the house strapped to a dolly by the two delivery women.
The woman moves forwards and says "Uh wait. Please." The delivery women stop wheeling out the old boyfriend.
There is a close up of the man's lifeless face as the woman kisses him.
The camera shows a close up the man's hand as the woman removes the ring from his finger.
The woman put the ring on the new man's finger, and places his hand back down on the dining room table.
There is a close up of the back of the new man's neck as the woman turns on the switch. The sound of the man powering on occurs. As the woman moves back to her chair to watch it occur.
The man's eyes blink and he looks around him. He grabs the napkin off of the table and places it on his lap.
The woman asks him "Glass of wine?" He responds: "No thanks, I've got a big client tomorrow". Then he takes a bite of his food.

The woman is silent. Soon the man comments "The asparagus is very tender." The woman smiles as she says "Yes dear." The man replies "They say it's an aphrodisiac, you know" before taking a bite of the asparagus.
The woman smiles again. She asks the man "Who does?". To which he tilts his head and responds "I don't know, just what people say."
The man rises from his seat and walks behind the woman.

He begins to massage her shoulders and says to her "So tell me about your day dear." She blinks a little, surprised. Then she replies "Oh, I went into town this morning to pick up some stuff for tonight..."
The camera cuts to a close up of the man's face as he looks down at the woman, listening.
The woman sighs enjoying the massage and continues: "...And um, I went for a light lunch with Anna".
The camera cuts to a close up of the man's hands as he massages the woman's shoulders. He says, "and then what?"
The woman closes her eyes and moans. She laughs a little before saying "And um then I decided since..."
The is another close up of the back of the woman's shoulders as he massages her. She continues to speak "...it's our anniversary, I decided to treat myself. And I"
We see a close up of the woman's face as she abruptly stops talking. The click of a switch can be heard, as the man shuts her off. Her head falls forward.
The man remains calm as he walks away. The woman's blank face is focused on as his footsteps retreat. The sound of a lighter can be heard.

The camera starts from the back of the woman, where a switch can be seen on her neck. It moves up to show the man sitting on the couch with a cigarette between his lips. He grabs the remote, puts his feet up and turns on the television.
The sound of cheering on the TV can be heard as he takes a puff of his cigarette.
A close up of his face is shown as he removes the cigarette from his lips and exhales smoke. His eyes look empty as he stares into the TV, and the scene fades to black.
The title THE SHOE appears on screen.
The screen opens to a close up of a shoe lying sideways on a sidewalk. The words HOW THEY GET THERE fade onto the corner of the screen. Music begins to play.
The shoe begins to move up from its side so that it is planted on the ground. As the music continues to play, the shoe begins to tap in time to the beat.
The camera shifts to a side ankle of the shoe. For a second it stops taping. A chips ahoy cookie wrapper comes into view as it falls to the ground.
The camera shifts to a close up of a milk carton. A hand slowly and deliberately opens the milk carton.
Focus shifts to a man's face. He is looking down and licks his lips.
The camera moves to a close up of the milk carton as the man peers into its opening. He then proceeds to take a drink from the milk.
The man pauses then takes another drink of his milk. He looks across the street, from where is his perched on his car, to see a woman walking down the sidewalk.
An older man walk outside from a house nearby. He approaches the guy leaning on his car before getting in the vehicle. No words are said, but the music continues to play.
The younger man walks away from the car. As he steps on the sidewalk from the street he trips, but soon recovers.
As the man is walking, he looks over at the woman. The camera cuts to her as she feigns tripping.
The camera cuts back to the man as he looks at the woman puzzled. He appears to slow down slightly.
The man puts down his milk carton at the top of a fence only for the woman to pretend to do the same on the other side of the street.
The camera cuts to the woman walking down the street after setting her fake milk carton down.
Moving back to the man, he begins to hunch over and swing his arms out in front of him.
The woman, glancing back over at the man, mimics his arm movements.
The man continues to look over at the woman as he does the same motion with his arms.
Cutting back to the woman, she slowly disappears from view (behind a metal box on the street). Emerging from this obstacle she acts out another movement.
The man, still swinging his arms, changes what he's doing to follow her.
The camera cuts back to the woman laughing and now hunched over holding her stomach.
The man now follows this motion as he approaches the end of the sidewalk at an intersection.

The woman suddenly stops and covers her mouth. The camera cuts to show a car approaching the intersection. The man, still looking at the woman, is oblivious. The woman tries to warn him about the car; however, he continues to mimic her.
The man walks right out into the intersection, and the car hits him.
The woman looks with her head slightly turned away at the scene. The car the has just hit the man goes airborne before it finally comes to a loud halt.
The woman looks up as the man's shoe comes flying through the sky. It hits the pavement on its side.
The camera moves up the side of the pavement, away from the first shoe. It soon comes across another type of shoe than the first.
The camera pauses on this second shoe as the music ends and the screen fades to black.
The title KEITH REYNOLDS fades on to the black screen.
The scene opens to a cartoon car approaching a tall building in the distance. Only the sound of the car's motor can be heard.
The scene cuts to write writing on a brown background. It says Keith Reynolds can't make it tonight
Camera cuts back to a close up of the car. A blue cartoon man gets out of the vehicle and slams the door shut.

Moving over to the building the man stops before the first step. He rubs his hand over his face and turns back around to the car. Having forgotten his hat, he goes back to retrieve it and once again slams the door shut.
As the man begins to walk towards the building music starts to play. A voice starts to introduce him: "This is Keith Reynolds, and today is promotion day."

Keith has reached the building. The camera view is one from the side of the building as if there was a wall missing. Four other people are talking inside on the ground floor The elevator reaches the bottom, as the music and introduction of the narrator continues: "Having worked at the company eight years, he's the most senior, junior analyst in the building. He's been waiting for this day, for a very long time." The elevator is held for Keith by another person as he enters, the doors shut, and the elevator begins its ascent.

The camera shows multiple floors as the elevator rises. The narrator continues: "When he gets his promotion, he will finally be a successful business man." The elevator stops on a floor with another woman. Keith gets off of the elevator.

The narrator says, "This is important because it means Sarah will like him." Keith walks towards Sarah who is bent over a filing cabinet. The elevator leaves the floor with another man in it. The narrator says, "That's her bending over now on the fourth floor..."

Keith grabs a cup from the stack by the fridge. The narrator adds, "...In an attempt to trigger the beginnings of her maybe wanting to spend the rest of her life with him. Keith Reynolds sometimes offers to make her a coffee." Keith presents the coffee to Sarah and she rejects it. The narrator describes "She never wants one."
Kieth walks away from Sarah and goes to pick up his briefcase. He walks towards the elevator, and the narrator finishes "...But Keith Reynolds always makes her a cup anyway."

The scene shifts moving down two floors. A man is sitting at his desk when suddenly someone outside falls down the ladder. The narrator shifts focus: "Meanwhile on the second floor, Bill from human resources has just seen something, or someone..."

The narrator continues with " fall past the window. A quick check confirms that it was someone, and he phones an ambulance." Bill gets up from his desk, looks out the window, and grabs his phone calling the ambulance.

Back on the fourth floor, Keith gets on the elevator and heads upstairs. The narrator describes, "Keith Reynold's reflection is looking confidently back at him from the shinny surface of the inside of the elevator" The elevator stops at a floor with a secretary and two other people shaking hands by the open door.

Keith exits the elevator, and walks over to the secretary. Meanwhile, the green man who had just been shaking hands with the red man, leaves the floor and heads for the elevator. The narrator describes Keith's thoughts: "He pictures the handshake in his head. The sound that the fat gold nib pen makes as he signs his name on the line"

The scene follows the green man's descent in the elevator, know revealed to be Patrick: "Patrick Campbell is slicking back his hair and straightening his tie. He just received some good news and is on his way to tell a special someone." Patrick has reached the fourth floor and exits walking towards Sarah.

The camera moves back up to the top floor where Keith has moved into the office of the boss. They shake hands, and the boss sits down. The narrator explains "The handshake was weaker than expected. The pen is nowhere in sight. The promotion: given to someone else."
Moving back down to the fourth floor, Patrick Campbell is hugging Sarah. The narrator begins, "That's him telling his good news to Sarah now on the fourth floor..."

The camera moves back up to the top floor where Keith has left the office and is moving towards the elevator. The narrator continues to speak as he gets on the elevator and heads towards the fourth floor: "The same floor Keith Reynolds is heading toward. Perhaps Sarah would like another cup of coffee, maybe tea, hot chocolate, anything Keith Reynolds could do to take-"

Keith has reached the fourth floor, and the elevator doors open. In front of him he sees Patrick and Sarah kissing. The narrator pauses as Keith Reynolds drops his briefcase. "They were kissing" Another pause. Then "The elevator door seemed to be staying open for an unusually long time. Their mouths were open.

The narrator describes Keith's thoughts as Patrick stops kissing Sarah and heads towards Keith: "Keith Reynold's fantasy of what he would do to Patrick Campbell if he were trapped in a small room with him was interrupted by the same Patrick Campbell asking him to hold the elevator door."

Patrick enters the elevator and they begin their ascent: "He felt himself take a deep breath. Keith Reynolds could see Patrick Campbell's reflection staring confidently back at him from the shiny surface of the inside of the elevator"
The elevator stops, having reached Patrick's stop. Patrick exits and the narrator continues: "He exhales his deep breath and as he does three words slip out..."

Patrick turns around and heads back towards the elevator to confront Keith. The narrator continues"...Keith Reynolds had never told anyone to do that to themselves before, and almost instantly wished he still hadn't. His apology was obviously insufficient"

Keith and Patrick begin to fight: "Some quick thinking was needed. Keith Reynolds wonders whether the punch to his face will result in bruising. He wonders if he's quick enough he can wash the blood out of his shirt before it stains."

Patrick is now lying limp on the floor. Keith continues to hit him until he is dead. The narrator describes the scene: "...whether Sarah likes a weatherman- a man's man. He just realized that he has killed Patrick Campbell. Some more quick thinking was needed"

The camera moves down a couple of floors. A woman appears by a filing cabinet. She is described as such: "Ruth Charles from accounts needs the toilet. Her favorite one in the building is on the seventh floor because it has a nicer soap dispenser. On account of her New Year's resolution, she takes the stairs."

The camera moves back up to Keith and Patrick, where Keith is dragging Patrick's body out of the elevator. The narrator adds, "Keith Reynolds decides to take Patrick home until he has time to dispose of him properly. He heads for the stairs. If someone calls the elevator on his way down, he'd be caught red-handed, and no one like a murderer."
The camera cuts back to Ruth walking up the stairs. "Ruth Charles has reached the sixth floor, and is also thinking about going home. Right up until she sees Keith Reynolds on the stairs..."

Keith continues to walk down the stairs until he sees Ruth. Ruth has paused, as the narrator describes Keith: "Keith Reynolds covered in blood and carrying a mutilated Patrick Campbell, sees Ruth's eyes roll in the back of her head.

Ruth falls down the stairs as she faints. A loud crack sounds before the narrator continues: "That was a genuine accident and Keith is sorry, but also careful to not step in her blood on his way past."

The camera cuts down to the fourth floor. Sarah grabs some files and heads towards the elevator. The narrator describes her thoughts, "It already feels like hours since Sarah last saw Patrick. So she's decided to take some files up to his office. As an excuse to see him again."

The camera moves up to where Keith is dragging Patrick's body: "After a quick rethink, Kieth Reynolds has decided to climb down the rather convenient ladder he has spotted outside the fifth floor window. That way he doesn't have to worry about passing security on the ground floor."
The camera cuts to the ground floor where security is talking. No narration, only music is heard.

The camera moves up to the fifth floor. Keith stops dragging Patrick's body as he reaches the window. The narrator begins to speak: "Sarah sees Keith. He thought about saying something but decided against it..."

As everyone stands there, the narrator continues with Keith's thoughts: "Maybe she liked the silent type. She looked at him in a way no woman had ever done before. He wished she would run into his arms. Kiss him and tell him she liked him just the way she was."
The narrator describes Sarah as she falls over: "She fainted. He guessed dead bodies had that effect on women"

Keith turns back towards the window, looks over the side, and the narrator continues: "Fortunately she was just clear of the stairs and would live to die another day. Keith Reynolds didn't know what he would do without her."
Keith is now on the ladder. He leans over to grab Patrick's body. "And as Keith tugged Patrick out onto the ladder, he suddenly realizes-" Patrick slips from Keith's grasp and falls.
Keith, balancing on the ladder continues to reflect "- that a lot of people probably want the same things he does: a great job, a beautiful wife, happiness. "

Keith continues to balance on the ladder, while inside the building a janitor is mopping the floor in front of him. "And if you're even lucky enough to get some of it, like Patrick down there, it only takes a moment and it can all be taken away from you."
Keith looks up at the window to see the janitor: "He takes one last look at the building, that has been his life for the last eight years, and sees a man he has never seen before."
The janitor stops mopping for a second, and looks up to see Keith outside of the window: "It was a rather surreal moment for Barry, whose job it was to mop the floors. Keith Reynolds balanced there."
